channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: RSL Australia supports Ben Roberts-Smith attending Anzac Day commemoration despite pending charges
item.description: RSL Australia has confirmed that Ben Roberts-Smith, a decorated veteran facing Commonwealth charges related to alleged conduct in Afghanistan, will be attending an Anzac Day ceremony in Queensland thi...

item.title: NMB Digital Systems Strengthen Government Revenue Collection and Financial Inclusion in Tanzania
item.description: NMB Bank has played a big role in improving government revenue collection in Tanzania through its digital payment platforms and financial services. According to Mr Innocent Yonazi, the bank’s Director...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules 31-Week Abortion Ban Violates Minor's Right to Life
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has delivered a significant ruling regarding abortion rights, particularly concerning a vulnerable minor. The court, while hearing a special leave petition, observed that fo...

item.title: NTSA to enforce mandatory safety checks on school transport vehicles before new term
item.description: The National Transport and Safety Authority (NTSA) has announced a fresh round of mandatory inspections targeting school buses and vans ahead of the reopening of schools. This move is part of its ongo...

item.title: Catholic 'Just War' Doctrine Critiques U.S. Actions in Iran Conflict
item.description: Pope Leo XIV’s consistent calls for peace amidst the ongoing Iran war have been met with criticism, particularly regarding the U.S. response. A growing number of Catholic voices are now invoking the '...

item.title: Kenyan Developer Builds Free Tool to Help Voters Access IEBC Offices and Registration Info
item.description: A Kenyan software developer, Simon Waithaka, has created a civic-tech platform called Mfumo to address challenges many voters face in Kenya, especially with finding the nearest IEBC office for voter r...

item.title: Intel Stock Rises 260% After Removal from Dow Jones Index
item.description: Intel Corporation's stock has surged 260% since its removal from the Dow Jones Industrial Average, highlighting a remarkable turnaround for the semiconductor giant. Intel was ousted from the prestigio...

item.title: When Immigrants Support Anti-Immigration Movements: A Look at Contradictions in Australian Conservatism
item.description: The article explores a striking aspect of Australian conservatism: immigrants who align themselves with anti-immigration rhetoric. Sami Shah focuses on Deboo Chatterjee, an Indian-born real estate pro...

item.title: Infinix NOTE 60 Ultra: A Game Changer for Premium Smartphones
item.description: Infinix has unveiled its flagship device, the NOTE 60 Ultra, which marks the company's bold entry into the premium smartphone market. Developed in collaboration with Pininfarina, a renowned Italian de...

item.title: Police officers investigated for alleged misconduct in Christopher Trybus manslaughter case
item.description: The Independent Office for Police Conduct (IOPC) has launched an investigation into police officers following concerns over their handling of the Christopher Trybus manslaughter case. The inquiry focu...

item.title: PDEA arrests shabu dealer and seizes 1 kilo of drugs in Marawi
item.description: Agents of the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region successfully conducted a buy-bust operation in Barangay Banga, Marawi City, on April 22, 2026, resulting in the sei...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal motorist fined R50,000 for exceeding speed limit by nearly double
item.description: A motorist in KwaZulu-Natal was recently slapped with a R50,000 fine after being caught driving at a shocking 215 km/h in a 120 km/h zone. Muhammeqqqqqd Khan appeared in the Scottburgh Magistrate’s Co...

item.title: Reflecting on the Importance of Anzac Day in Contemporary Australia
item.description: This editorial emphasises the renewed significance of Anzac Day in 2026, highlighting both global and domestic contexts that make remembrance particularly poignant. With ongoing conflicts in Ukraine a...

item.title: Pappu Yadav Defends Controversial Remarks on Women, Refuses to Apologise
item.description: Rajesh Ranjan, known as Pappu Yadav, an Independent MP from Bihar's Purnea, sparked controversy over his comments on the Women’s Reservation Bill and the exploitation of women in politics. Yadav firml...

item.title: Canada prepares for megawatt EV charging, starting with a truck pilot in Quebec
item.description: CBC’s Earth Week newsletter looks at fast EV charging, sargassum, and conservation stories, with the main feature focused on megawatt charging technology. The article explains that BYD has developed u...

item.title: Los Angeles Police Investigate Home Invasion Where Elderly Woman Was Strangled and Robbed
item.description: Police in Los Angeles are investigating a violent home invasion that took place Thursday night in the Hollywood Hills. According to the Los Angeles Police Department, two suspects entered a residence ...

item.title: African tribal leader removed from Scottish woods and deported from the UK
item.description: A leader of an African tribe, who had been residing in the woods of Scotland under what he described as the Kingdom of Kubala, has been deported from the United Kingdom following an eviction. Authorit...

item.title: Head teacher dies after being hit by a car in Altrincham
item.description: A man has been formally charged with the murder of Michael Mullins, a 56-year-old head teacher at Stretford Grammar School, who tragically died after being struck by a vehicle while cycling home from ...

item.title: U.S. Watchdog Investigates Compliance with Epstein Files Release Law
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice's internal watchdog is currently reviewing the compliance of the department with a law requiring the release of files related to Jeffrey Epstein. This move comes in resp...

item.title: Duterte's Lawyer Urged to Focus on Case, Stop Commenting on Philippine Politics
item.description: The Presidential Palace has rebuked lawyer Nicholas Kaufman, representing former President Rodrigo Duterte, for publicly commenting on Philippine politics instead of focusing on Duterte’s legal defens...
